PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Sep-08-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught for (petitioner) Paul Lillig-Gruesser.

The Court stated that it has received the withdrawal of objections and granted the petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary (subject to review of the order).

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Fender. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-27-2016 Continued To Sep-07-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Jon Vaught for (petitioner) Paul Lillig-Gruesser. Robert Bergman (via court call) for (objector) Susan Cassling. David Dupont (objector) in Propria Persona.

Mr. Vaught reported that he just received the objections yesterday, that the parties have no standing to object and that the only beneficiaries are the decedents husband and son. Mr. Bergman stated that they do have standing to object to the validity of the will, that Ms. Cassling was named as the executor of the estate, that petitioner claims she waived her right as she never filed for probate and that she was told that there was nothing to probate. Mr. Vaught stated that she has no standing to attack the validity of the will, that regarding the issue of the petition she waived her right and has done nothing to defend the will. Mr. Bergman stated that they disagree regarding the waiving of rights by attacking the will, that he has not had a chance to confer with Mr. Vaught and would like that opportunity. Mr. Dupont stated that he objects and echo's what has been stated. The Court stated that the parties can be referred to mediation or do so informally and that the will contest needs a summons. Mr. Vaught stated that he thinks the court should strike the objection and move on the petition to admit the will to probate. Mr. Bergman stated that they are fine with mediation but thinks informal discussions would be appropriate. Mr. Vaught stated that he is not sure what needs to be worked out. Mr. Bergman stated that the petitioner says that he just found the will but that they have an email from 2007 stating that he had found the will then. Mr. Vaught stated that they have no standing to be here. The Court asked Mr. Bergman if he intends to file anything regarding their standing, that it will continue the Petition for Probate of Will and for Letters Testamentary to September 7, 2016 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, that the parties should engage in informal discussions, that Mr. Bergman and Mr. Dupont should file any responses no later than August 24, 2016 and that Mr. Vaught can file a responsive brief if he wants by August 31, 2016.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jul-06-2016 Continued To Jul-27-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
